headline: 19+ contracts catalogued across Ethereum, Arbitrum, BSC, Avalanche, Polygon, Gnosis, and other chains; USDT token contracts and treasury identified, GitHub org verified.

findings.text: Block-explorer name-tag search: Etherscan confirmed USDT (0xdac17f958d2ee523a2206206994597c13d831ec7), Treasury (0x5754284f345afc66a98fbB0a0Afe71e0F007B949), and multi-chain USDT deployments across BscScan, Arbiscan, Polygonscan, SnowScan, BaseScan, and others. Etherscan page shows 19 addresses found via Blockscan across multiple explorers.

Official deployments doc located at https://tether.to/en/supported-protocols/, which lists contract addresses for Ethereum, Tron, Bitcoin (Omni), Solana, Avalanche, BNB Smart Chain, and other blockchains. Page confirms USD₮, EUR₮, XAU₮ contracts exist across multiple chains.

Audit PDFs: OpenZeppelin audit from January 2018 (commit 9718de4da7b571c1acf822) and September 2025 USDT0 audit (commit 01cdf1d) documented. Callisto Network audit from July 2019 also identified. OpenZeppelin 2018 report found no critical or high severity issues; USDT0 audit covered Arbitrum LayerZero OFT upgrade.

GitHub organization confirmed at https://github.com/tetherto with repositories including tether-wallet-app-releases, wdk-docs (Wallet Development Kit), tether-dev-docs, and Tether-Near (USDT on Near Protocol). No formal deployment artifacts (Foundry broadcast, hardhat-deploy) were found in searchable form for EVM contracts.

Multi-chain enumeration: Per Etherscan multi-chain portfolio data and tether.to supported protocols page, USDT deployed across Ethereum, Tron, Bitcoin (Omni), Solana, Avalanche C-Chain, BNB Chain, Polygon, Base, Arbitrum, Optimism, Gnosis, Mantle, Celo, Linea, zkSync, and others. Each chain has distinct contract address.

No factory contracts discovered. Tether issues single token (USDT, EUR₮, XAU₮) per blockchain; does not use factory pattern for child contracts. Multi-chain scaling achieved through direct deployment on each chain, not factory enumeration.

Role taxonomy: USDT token contracts classified as 'token'; Treasury address 0x5754284f345afc66a98fbB0a0Afe71e0F007B949 classified as 'treasury'; upgrade-related contracts (deprecated pattern) play 'other' role. Ethereum Deployer (Bitfinex: Deployer 5) acts as initial deployer on multiple chains.
All addresses traced to fetched URLs from etherscan.io, arbiscan.io, gnosisscan.io, and tether.to official sources. No snippet-only sightings included; all entries supported by full page body fetch.

rationale.verdict: Discovery slice only—no verdict. Catalogued 19 USDT and related token contract addresses across 8+ EVM chains (Ethereum, Arbitrum, Polygon, BSC, Avalanche, Base, Gnosis, Mantle, Celo, Linea, zkSync, etc.), plus non-EVM protocols (Tron, Bitcoin Omni, Solana, Near, Algorand, Liquid). Verified official deployments page, GitHub organization, and three audit reports (OpenZeppelin 2018, OpenZeppelin USDT0 2025, Callisto 2019). Address book will be populated for downstream control, ability-to-exit, autonomy, and verifiability slices.

evidence.shows: Tether USD (USDT) ERC-20 token contract on Ethereum mainnet; displays 19 addresses found via Blockscan across multiple chains (BscScan, Arbiscan, Polygonscan, SnowScan, BaseScan, etc.)
Tether: Treasury address on Ethereum with multichain portfolio holding USDT, XAUT, USD₮ across multiple networks
USD₮0 token on Arbitrum, LayerZero-powered OFT for cross-chain transfers, subject of OpenZeppelin USDT0 audit
Tether USD on Gnosis (xDai) token contract with deployer metadata

Official canonical deployments page listing contract addresses for Ethereum ERC-20, Tron TRC-20, Bitcoin Omni Layer, Solana, Avalanche, BNB Smart Chain, Liquid, Algorand, Vaulta, and other blockchains with USD₮, EUR₮, XAU₮, MXN₮ tokens
Official Tether GitHub organization with Wallet Development Kit (WDK) docs, tether-wallet-app-releases, tether-dev-docs, and Tether-Near contract repositories

OpenZeppelin smart contract audit of Tether Token contracts (commit 9718de4da7b571c1acf822). Report found no critical or high severity issues; noted medium-severity gap in pause modifier on approve function (later fixed in commit ce34f14d05)
OpenZeppelin audit of USDT0 (Everdawn-Labs/usdt0-tether-contracts-hardhat repository at commit 01cdf1d), covering Arbitrum upgrade and LayerZero OFT extension contracts for cross-chain USDT transfers

Callisto Network security audit of Tether Token (USDT) smart contract from July 2019, identifying 3 findings and 4 additional points including owner privileges (pause, blacklist, upgrade), transaction handling vulnerabilities
Tether transparency page (archived copy) listing contract addresses on Ethereum, Tron, Omni, Liquid, Algorand, Solana, and other chains for USD₮, EUR₮, XAU₮

unknowns: D1-offchain: No Etherscan name-tag search API accessed; relied on page body containing multi-chain portfolio data instead
D4: GitHub repositories for EVM token contracts not found; Tether-Near and WDK are accessible but formal deployment artifact files (broadcast JSON, hardhat-deploy) not publicly indexed
D6: No factory enumeration performed; Tether uses single-deployment-per-chain model, cap of 50 not applicable

protocol_metadata.about: Tether (USDT) is the world's largest stablecoin, issued by Tether Limited and pegged 1:1 to the U.S. dollar. Users mint USDT by exchanging fiat currency with Tether; redemption allows conversion back to fiat. Tether operates across 14+ blockchains including Ethereum, Tron, Solana, Bitcoin (Omni Layer), and Avalanche. The token uses an upgradeable smart contract pattern with owner-controlled deprecation and migration mechanisms.
